title: News of the day: Collapsing rental market, Shell buys ARC, sovereign wealth fund, Bank of Canada preview, government spending and more
body: It’s Monday, April 27. Here are the top stories we’re following today. How a collapsing rental market is costing some homeowners more than they bargained for Buyers who entered the market at its peak on the hopes of renting out their property to help pay off their mortgage are now facing a different reality. Read the story Oil giant Shell to buy Canada’s ARC Resources for $22 billion Shell PLC has agreed to buy Canadian oil and gas producer Arc Resources Ltd. for $22 billion. The deal, still subject to regulatory approvals, offers shareholders 0.40 shares in Shell and $8.20 in cash for every ARC share, which the company says amounts to about a 27 per cent premium. title: Former president of Costa Rica on de-risking fertilizer shocks: how $700 billion in subsidies can do more
body: Oops, something went wrong In 2022, my final year in office, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine sent fertilizer prices surging several-fold, and farmers across Central America—and around the world—saw production costs spike almost overnight, raising fears of food shortages. Today those fears have resurfaced. Conflict in the Middle East is rattling energy markets, pushing up the cost of natural gas—the backbone of nitrogen fertilizer production—and exposing once again just how vulnerable farmers and families are to shocks beyond their control. Fertilizer prices have skyrocketed by up to 40% , compromising livelihoods and food security. This vulnerability is not accidental and it is not cheap either. Governments spend more than $700 billion a year subsidising agriculture, much of it bankrolling

title: How Investors May Respond To Comstock Resources (CRK) Backing NextEra’s Texas Gas Power Hub
body: Oops, something went wrong In March 2026, Comstock Resources disclosed that its Western Haynesville operations would support the newly designated Texas Natural Gas-Fired Power Generation Hub in Anderson County, a NextEra Energy-led project targeting up to 5.2 GW of gas-fired capacity. The collaboration positions Comstock to potentially supply close to 1 Bcf of natural gas per day by 2031, a scale that could materially influence its long-term sales profile and capital planning. Next, we’ll consider how Comstock’s potential role supplying nearly 1 Bcf per day to this power hub could reshape its investment narrative. title: Shell is Acquiring a Canadian Energy Company for Nearly $14 Billion. Here's What it Could Mean for the Global LNG Market.
body: Shell ( SHEL 2.49% ) is making a big splash. The global integrated energy giant has agreed to acquire Canadian energy company ARC Resources ( ARX +21.15% ) for $13.6 billion. The deal will significantly increase Shell's oil and gas production rate through 2030. The multi-billion-dollar deal comes amid major upheaval in global energy markets. The Strait of Hormuz closure by Iran has disrupted the flow of oil and liquified natural gas (LNG) out of the Persian Gulf. The deal enhances Shell's ability to supply the world with more LNG outside of the Gulf in the future. Image source: Getty Images. Shell has agreed to buy ARC Resources in a cash-and-stock deal valuing the Canadian energy company at $13.6 billion, with the total value rising to $16.4 billion when accounting for ARC's net debt and
